name: litellm-vscode-chat

litellm-vscode-chat.defaultMaxOutputToke.description: Default maximum output tokens when not provided by model info

litellm-vscode-chat.defaultContextLength.description: Default context length when not provided by model info

litellm-vscode-chat.defaultMaxInputToken.description: Default maximum input tokens (if null, calculated as contextLength - maxOutputTokens)

litellm-vscode-chat.promptCaching.enable.markdownDeprecationMessage: **Deprecated.** Use `#litellm-vscode-chat.promptCaching.mode#` instead (`off` | `chat` | `agent` | `auto`). For backward compatibility, a value of `false` here is still honoured as `mode: "off"` only when `promptCaching.mode` has not been explicitly set.
litellm-vscode-chat.promptCaching.enable.deprecationMessage: Deprecated: use litellm-vscode-chat.promptCaching.mode instead. A value of false is still honoured as mode 'off' when mode is not explicitly set.

enumDescriptions: Disable prompt caching entirely. No cache_control breakpoints are sent.

Optimise for interactive chat: place eligible cache breakpoints (tools, system, first user message, rolling-last) at the default 5-minute TTL. Static anchors below promptCaching.minCacheTokens are skipped. Best for short bursty conversations where a longer-lived cache would rarely be reused before it expires anyway.

Optimise for long agent runs: cache eligible stable-head anchors (system prompt, first user message, tools array) at the extended 1-hour TTL, and the volatile rolling-last anchor at 5 minutes. Static anchors below promptCaching.minCacheTokens are skipped. Best when a session spans many tool round-trips over a long wall-clock window.

Default. Cache stable anchors (system prompt, first user message, tools array) at 1 hour once they meet promptCaching.minCacheTokens, and cache the rolling-last anchor at 5 minutes. promptCaching.tokenSizeAutoBreakpoint is retained for compatibility and no longer affects TTL selection.
litellm-vscode-chat.promptCaching.mode.description: Prompt-caching strategy for supported models (currently Anthropic Claude). Controls the cache TTL ('5m' vs extended '1h') applied to each cache_control breakpoint. Only affects models that advertise prompt caching support via LiteLLM's /v1/model/info endpoint. Note: the '1h' tier requires your LiteLLM gateway to forward the extended-cache-ttl beta to the backend; if it does not, those anchors silently fall back to 5 minutes.

litellm-vscode-chat.promptCaching.tokenS.description: Deprecated / no longer affects TTL selection. In 'auto' mode all stable anchors (system, first user message, tools) now use the 1-hour TTL once they clear the minCacheTokens floor, because a 1h cache read costs the same as 5m and refreshes for free. Whether an anchor is cached at all is governed by 'minCacheTokens', not this setting. Retained for backward compatibility.

litellm-vscode-chat.promptCaching.minCac.description: Minimum estimated block size (in tokens) for a cache_control breakpoint to be placed at all, in every mode. Blocks below this floor are not tagged because the provider will not cache a prefix shorter than its minimum cacheable length. The default (4096) matches the minimum cacheable prefix of the latest Anthropic Claude models (e.g. Opus 4.5+/4.7); older models accept as low as 1024. Also suppresses caching of a tiny or absent system prompt.

enumDescriptions: Always tag the last message of every request with a cache_control breakpoint. Maximum cache coverage but most fragile: in long agent runs, a tool result whose bytes change between turns (truncation, embedded timestamps, file edits) will silently invalidate the rolling-last anchor and force a full cache miss on the next turn.

Default. Tag the last message only when it is plain user text, plain assistant text, or an assistant turn carrying tool_calls. Skip the breakpoint when the last message is a tool result (role: 'tool'), which is the most common cause of full cache misses inside the TTL window in tool-heavy agent sessions. Recommended for long agent runs that read large files or capture lots of terminal output.

Never tag a rolling-last breakpoint. Keep only the static anchors (tools + system + first user message). Lowest cache coverage but most predictable; useful for debugging or when you suspect the rolling anchor is misbehaving.
litellm-vscode-chat.promptCaching.rollin.description: Where to place the rolling-last cache_control breakpoint on the most recent message of every request. Orthogonal to promptCaching.mode (which controls the TTL); this controls placement. Has no effect when promptCaching.mode is 'off' or the model lacks supports_prompt_caching.

litellm-vscode-chat.modelParameters.description: Model-specific request parameters to customize API behavior. Supports max_tokens, temperature, top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, stop, etc. Uses longest prefix matching (e.g., 'gpt-4' matches 'gpt-4-turbo:openai'). For multi-server setups, prefix with the server label to scope parameters to a specific server (e.g., 'Production/gpt-4'). Server-scoped entries take priority over unscoped ones. Set '_replaceDefaults: true' in a model entry to skip built-in codebase defaults (e.g., temperature) for that model. Note: gpt-5.5 has no built-in default temperature.

litellm-vscode-chat.maskApiKeyInput.description: Mask the API key input field when configuring. Disable to see the key as you type.

litellm-vscode-chat.requestTimeout.description: Request timeout in milliseconds for chat completion requests. Default is 300000ms (5 minutes). Increase this value if you're experiencing timeout errors with long-running requests.

litellm-vscode-chat.discoveryTimeout.description: Request timeout in milliseconds for model discovery requests. Default is 30000ms (30 seconds).

description: An extension that integrates LiteLLM into GitHub Copilot Chat
displayName: LiteLLM Provider for GitHub Copilot Chat
